evals.query: Set up the Kora BOM in build.gradle for a Java service with the annotation processor.
expected_behavior: Declares a koraBom configuration and makes annotationProcessor, compileOnly, and implementation extendsFrom(koraBom)
Imports the BOM via koraBom platform("ru.tinkoff.kora:kora-parent:<version>")
Adds annotationProcessor "ru.tinkoff.kora:annotation-processors"
References Kora modules without an explicit version
Does not use foreign-framework annotations, field injection, or a foreign-framework starter dependency

evals.query: Configure a Kotlin Kora project with KSP and the BOM. Kotlin is 1.9.25.
expected_behavior: Applies the com.google.devtools.ksp plugin with a version matching Kotlin (1.9.25-1.0.20)
Adds ksp("ru.tinkoff.kora:symbol-processors")
Makes the ksp configuration extendsFrom(koraBom)
Imports the BOM via koraBom(platform("ru.tinkoff.kora:kora-parent:<version>"))
Does not add the Java annotationProcessor dependency for the Kotlin source set

evals.query: Which Kora dependencies do I need for a REST API with an HTTP server, JSON, and metrics?
expected_behavior: Adds ru.tinkoff.kora:http-server-undertow
Adds ru.tinkoff.kora:json-module
Adds ru.tinkoff.kora:micrometer-module for metrics and notes that the Prometheus scrape is served on the private HTTP port
Includes core modules config-hocon and logging-logback
Does not invent a non-existent artifact such as http-server-common, micrometer-prometheus, or probes

evals.query: Add dependencies for a Kora JDBC service backed by PostgreSQL with Flyway migrations.
expected_behavior: Adds ru.tinkoff.kora:database-jdbc
Adds ru.tinkoff.kora:database-flyway for SQL migrations
Adds the PostgreSQL driver org.postgresql:postgresql with an explicit version because it is not in the BOM
Notes that JDBC transactions are part of database-jdbc (no separate transaction artifact)
Does not reference invented artifacts such as jdbc-driver, transaction-jdbc, or migrate-kora

evals.query: I need a Kora Kafka producer and consumer with JSON. Which module and what does the build look like?
expected_behavior: Adds the single ru.tinkoff.kora:kafka artifact for both producer and consumer
Adds ru.tinkoff.kora:json-module for serialization
Mentions @KafkaPublisher for producing and @KafkaListener for consuming
Does not add separate kafka-producer or kafka-consumer artifacts (they do not exist)
Does not suggest a foreign-framework Kafka starter

evals.query: I have a Jackson version conflict. How do I let the Kora BOM manage Jackson instead of pinning it myself?
expected_behavior: Explains that the kora-parent BOM pins Jackson and other transitive libraries
Recommends removing the explicit jackson-databind version declaration
Recommends depending on ru.tinkoff.kora:json-module and letting the BOM resolve Jackson
Mentions resolutionStrategy.force only as a last resort for a genuine conflict
Does not advise pinning a raw Jackson version on top of the BOM

evals.query: Add the Kora resilience module so I can use @CircuitBreaker and @Retry.
expected_behavior: Adds ru.tinkoff.kora:resilient-kora (the real artifact name)
Mentions the @Retry, @CircuitBreaker, @Timeout, and @Fallback annotations
Keeps the BOM responsible for the version (no version on the artifact)
Does not use the incorrect artifact name resilient
Does not pull in Resilience4j directly or use a foreign-framework retry library

evals.query: Set up a multi-module Gradle build so every submodule shares one Kora version.
expected_behavior: Applies the BOM in a subprojects block (or convention) so each leaf module imports kora-parent
Wires annotationProcessor/ksp via extendsFrom(koraBom) in each module that generates code
Pins koraVersion once in gradle.properties
Mentions @KoraSubmodule to export components from a submodule into the @KoraApp
Does not version individual ru.tinkoff.kora:* artifacts per module

evals.query: Which dependency gives me a Kora S3 client for AWS?
expected_behavior: Uses the experimental group: ru.tinkoff.kora.experimental:s3-client-aws
Mentions the @S3.Client declarative client and the AwsS3ClientModule
Notes that S3 modules are in the experimental group, not ru.tinkoff.kora
Does not invent a ru.tinkoff.kora:s3-client or s3-client-ceph artifact

evals.query: Scaffold a new Kora Java service with an HTTP server and PostgreSQL using the project generator.
expected_behavior: Runs scripts/generate_project.py with --lang java and --modules including http-server and jdbc-postgres
Produces a build.gradle importing the kora-parent BOM and the chosen modules
Generates an @KoraApp interface that extends the module interfaces and runs KoraApplication.run(ApplicationGraph::graph)
Generates a controller using @HttpController and @HttpRoute and a repository using @Repository extends JdbcRepository
Generated code does not contain foreign-framework package imports or field injection
